title: Azure Stack HCI Cluster Validation, Quorum Health, and Witness Reachability
exclusions: Azure Stack Hub AKS-HCI lifecycle and disconnected stamps whose quorum tooling differs from Azure Stack HCI on validated OEM hardware; Linux Pacemaker or RHVH clusters; VMware vSAN-only footprints without Windows Failover Clustering; Hyper-V standalone hosts not joined to an Azure Stack HCI cluster; Azure VMware Solution (AVS) estates where VMware—not FailoverClusters cmdlets—defines resilience.
dataSources: `index=azure_stack_hci` (or `hci_cluster`, `windows`, `wineventlog` per naming convention) combining Universal Forwarder WinEventLog inputs from `Microsoft-Windows-FailoverClustering/Operational` (cluster service, witness, quorum arbitration—event IDs include **1135** node removed from membership, **1146** RHS failure, **1554** quorum arbitration failed, **1561–1562** witness online/offline transitions, **1564** file-share witness share inaccessible, **1566** cloud witness unreachable, **1571** CSV arbitration failure, **1606** quorum vote configuration changed), `Microsoft-Windows-ClusterAwareUpdating/Admin` for CAU validation phases that rerun `Test-Cluster` subsets, `Microsoft-Windows-StorageSpaces-Driver/Operational` when Storage Spaces Direct ties into stretch layouts, optional `Microsoft-Windows-Storage-Storport/Admin` for SCSI timeouts affecting witness disks. Scripted JSON HEC or modular collection emitting `sourcetype=azurestackhci:cluster` with fields distilled from `Get-ClusterQuorum`, `Get-ClusterNode`, `Test-Cluster` report aggregation (`-ReportName` HTML/XML paths), `Test-ClusterValidationLog`, `Get-ClusterLog` extracts (RHS/NetFT sections). Azure Monitor feeds via Splunk_TA_microsoft-cloudservices (Splunkbase **3110**) and Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Azure (Splunkbase **3757**) for `azure:resource_health`, `azure:arc:vm` heartbeat skew—tie-break context only; Splunk_TA_windows (Splunkbase **742**) owns parsing defaults for WinEventLog.
app: **`Splunk_TA_windows`** ([Splunkbase 742](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)) on each Azure Stack HCI node or Heavy Forwarder aggregating forwarded WinEventLog; **`Splunk_TA_microsoft-cloudservices`** ([Splunkbase 3110](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3110)) for Azure Monitor / Log Analytics bridges; **Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Azure** ([Splunkbase 3757](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3757)) for ARM microsoft.azurestackhci/clusters resource metrics where deployed; optional dedicated custom app wrapping **`azurestackhci_validate.ps1`** (scheduled **`interval = 86400`**) emitting **`azurestackhci:cluster`** JSON lines.
spl:
(`index=azure_stack_hci` OR index=hci_cluster OR index=wineventlog OR index=windows)
(`sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-FailoverClustering/Operational"` OR `sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-ClusterAwareUpdating/Admin"` OR `sourcetype="azurestackhci:cluster"`)
`earliest=-24h`
| eval evt_code=tostring(coalesce(EventCode, event_id, EventID, "unknown"))
| eval witness_evt=case(
    evt_code IN ("1564","1566"), "witness_transport",
    evt_code IN ("1561","1562"), "witness_state",
    evt_code IN ("1554","1571"), "quorum_failure",
    evt_code="1135", "membership_loss",
    evt_code="1146", "rhs_resource_fault",
    evt_code="1606", "vote_config_changed",
    1=1, "other_cluster")
| eval scripted_quorum_risk=case(
    match(_raw, "(?i)witness_unreachable.?[:=].?(true|1)") OR match(_raw, "(?i)\"witness_reachable\"\\s*:\\s*false"), "scripted_unreachable",
    match(_raw, "(?i)validation_status.?[:=].?(fail|warning)") OR match(_raw, "(?i)\"validation_overall\"\\s*:\\s*\"(Failed|Warning)\""), "validation_bad",
    sourcetype="azurestackhci:cluster" AND isnotnull(quorum_resource_type) AND (witness_latency_ms>800 OR witness_https_ok="false"), "metric_threshold",
    null())
| eval quorum_resource_type=coalesce(quorum_resource_type, witness_type, "unknown")
| eval cluster_name=upper(trim(coalesce(cluster_name, Cluster_Name, fq_cluster, ComputerName)))
| where witness_evt IN ("witness_transport","witness_state","quorum_failure") OR scripted_quorum_risk IS NOT NULL OR evt_code IN ("1554","1571")
| stats latest(_time) as last_evt,
       values(evt_code) as codes,
       values(witness_evt) as witness_paths,
       values(quorum_resource_type) as quorum_modes,
       max(case(scripted_quorum_risk IN ("validation_bad","scripted_unreachable","metric_threshold"),1,0)) as scripted_alert,
       dc(host) as reporting_nodes
    by cluster_name
| eval posture=case(
    scripted_alert=1 OR mvfind(witness_paths,"quorum_failure")>0, "immediate_review",
    mvfind(witness_paths,"witness_transport")>0 OR mvfind(witness_paths,"witness_state")>0, "witness_flap",
    1=1, "informational")
| eval quorum_modes=mvdedup(quorum_modes)
| sort -last_evt
| table cluster_name, posture, quorum_modes, witness_paths, codes, scripted_alert, reporting_nodes, last_evt
description: Detects Windows Failover Cluster integrity erosion on Azure Stack HCI—failed Test-Cluster / ClusterValidationLog outcomes, unstable Get-ClusterQuorum witness roles (Cloud Witness, File Share Witness, legacy disk-only patterns), NetFT heartbeat stress between nodes, mis-prioritized cluster-only networks, and stretch-cluster scenarios where Storage Replica partnerships plus vote math demand a healthy third-site tie breaker.
value: Quorum loss pauses clustered VMs, blocks Storage Spaces Direct rebuild arbitration, and turns stretch replication into a split-brain gamble. Surfacing Test-Cluster regressions beside Microsoft-Windows-FailoverClustering/Operational witness telemetry lets operators restore majority before the second fault—not after tenants notice hung live migrations.
implementation: Forward FailoverClustering/Operational channels through TA parsing; ship nightly azurestackhci:cluster JSON snapshots from Get-ClusterQuorum, Get-ClusterNode, and Test-Cluster wrappers; correlate scripted witness_https_ok probes (Test-NetConnection to *.blob.core.windows.net port 443) with Event IDs 1566 and 1564; chart Cluster Network adapter priority versus Microsoft Failover Cluster Virtual Adapter messaging embedded in validation logs.
visualization: (1) Single-value tiles: clusters flagged immediate_review versus witness_flap over trailing 24h. (2) Timeline: last_evt per cluster_name colored by posture. (3) Table: quorum_modes, witness_paths, codes, reporting_nodes. (4) Choropleth optional for geo-separated stretch pairs using CMDB site tags—not Splunk-native unless enriched. (5) Drill dashboard tying azurestackhci:cluster scripted validation_overall strings to vendor validate docs.

references.title: Validate an Azure Stack HCI cluster (Microsoft Learn)
Deploy a cloud witness for a failover cluster (Microsoft Learn)
Stretch clustering with Storage Replica on Azure Stack HCI (Microsoft Learn)
Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (Splunkbase 3110)

knownFalsePositives: **Rolling quorum adjustments** during Invoke-CauRun while DynamicQuorum reshuffles NodeWeight mid-drain—Event **1606** looks benign yet paging lacks CAU phase tags unless enrichment lookups supply MaintenanceWindow IDs tied to Invoke-CauRun job names. **Long-running Test-Cluster suites** (**Inventory + Network + Storage + Hyper-V Configuration**) overlap nightly quiet hours; scripted validation_overall=Warning rows surface before engineers finish HTML triage—suppress until ClusterValidationLog severity escalates beyond informational-only findings. **Azure Blob fabric refreshes** occasionally inflate witness_https_ok latency toward HTTPS timeouts absent HCI faults—cross-check bursts of Event **1566** against azure:resource_health storage incidents before paging storage SMEs. **Stretch rehearsal drills** that Stop-Service ClusSvc at one site duplicate Event **1135** storms across observers—exclude rehearsal hosts via CMDB rehearsal=true markers so quorum drills never mimic outages. **NetFT silence during converged NIC QoS edits** resembles quorum distress until Get-ClusterNetwork priorities stabilize—pair Splunk alerts with Network ATC intent logs on Windows Server **2025** automated deployments. **Antivirus pauses on File Share Witness UNC paths** mimic ACL-loss signatures identical to transient Event **1564** spikes—confirm NTFS Cluster Name Object ACE audits rather than reacting solely to Splunk spikes.
detailedImplementation:
**Prerequisites**
• **Splunk Universal Forwarder** on each Azure Stack HCI node (**23H2** GA preferred; **22H2 LTS** still common; note **21H2** exited mainstream support September **2024**) with **Splunk_TA_windows** (**Splunkbase 742**) applying props/transforms defaults so WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-FailoverClustering/Operational retains EventCode, TaskCategory, ComputerName, and Message blobs intact.

• **Heavy Forwarder** or deployment server staging directory hosting azurestackhci_validate.ps1—bundle FailoverClusters module calls (`Test-Cluster -Node … -ReportName`, `Save-CauDebugTrace` during CAU tie-ins), `Get-ClusterQuorum`, `Get-ClusterNode | Format-Table Name,State,DynamicWeight`, `Get-ClusterResource` scoped to Cluster Group, optional `Test-NetConnection` toward *.blob.core.windows.net (**443/tcp**) for Cloud Witness, UNC paths such as \\fs01\witness for File Share Witness, Azure Files endpoints when applicable.

• **Splunk HTTP Event Collector** token scoped to azurestackhci:cluster sourcetype (**TLS mutual-auth** recommended); rotate storage account keys whenever Set-ClusterQuorum -CloudWitness secrets rotate.

• **Network/security:** nodes must reach *.blob.core.windows.net (**Azure Public**), *.blob.core.usgovcloudapi.net (**Azure Gov**), or *.blob.core.chinacloudapi.cn (**Azure China**) per topology—HTTPS intercept appliances frequently distort witness latency probes aligned with Event **1566**.

• **RBAC:** roles running saved searches need azure_stack_hci plus mirrored wineventlog indexes declared in **authorize.conf**.

Step 1 — **Configure ingestion paths**
(1) Enable **inputs.conf** stanzas [WinEventLog://Microsoft-Windows-FailoverClustering/Operational], [WinEventLog://Microsoft-Windows-ClusterAwareUpdating/Admin], optional **Storage Spaces** channels when validating stretch pools tied to Storage Replica baselines.

(2) Deploy azurestackhci_validate.ps1 under bin/ inside your custom TA with interval = 86400 (**weekly Test-Cluster cadence**—full suites often 30–90 minutes; stagger nodes). Serialize JSON containing cluster_name, quorum_resource_type (Cloud Witness, File Share Witness, Disk Witness, No Majority variants), witness_latency_ms, witness_https_ok, validation_overall (Passed/Failed/Warning), netft_heartbeat_seconds snapshots parsed from Get-ClusterLog NetFt excerpts.

(3) Install **Splunk_TA_microsoft-cloudservices** plus **Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Azure** where Azure Arc heartbeat drift influences Cloud Witness correlation against azure:arc:vm telemetry.

(4) Document stretch-cluster Storage Replica topology (`Get-SRPartnership`) yet treat replication KPI depth under sibling capacity use cases—here partnership facts feed quorum interpretation only (third-site witness mandate).

Step 2 — **Author searches and macros**
Reuse the SPL packaged in the spl field once evt_code extraction parity is confirmed across TA builds—some deployments lowercase event_id instead of EventCode.

```spl
(`index=azure_stack_hci` OR index=hci_cluster OR index=wineventlog OR index=windows)
(`sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-FailoverClustering/Operational"` OR `sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-ClusterAwareUpdating/Admin"` OR `sourcetype="azurestackhci:cluster"`)
`earliest=-24h`
| eval evt_code=tostring(coalesce(EventCode, event_id, EventID, "unknown"))
| eval witness_evt=case(
    evt_code IN ("1564","1566"), "witness_transport",
    evt_code IN ("1561","1562"), "witness_state",
    evt_code IN ("1554","1571"), "quorum_failure",
    evt_code="1135", "membership_loss",
    evt_code="1146", "rhs_resource_fault",
    evt_code="1606", "vote_config_changed",
    1=1, "other_cluster")
| eval scripted_quorum_risk=case(
    match(_raw, "(?i)witness_unreachable.?[:=].?(true|1)") OR match(_raw, "(?i)\"witness_reachable\"\\s*:\\s*false"), "scripted_unreachable",
    match(_raw, "(?i)validation_status.?[:=].?(fail|warning)") OR match(_raw, "(?i)\"validation_overall\"\\s*:\\s*\"(Failed|Warning)\""), "validation_bad",
    sourcetype="azurestackhci:cluster" AND isnotnull(quorum_resource_type) AND (witness_latency_ms>800 OR witness_https_ok="false"), "metric_threshold",
    null())
| eval quorum_resource_type=coalesce(quorum_resource_type, witness_type, "unknown")
| eval cluster_name=upper(trim(coalesce(cluster_name, Cluster_Name, fq_cluster, ComputerName)))
| where witness_evt IN ("witness_transport","witness_state","quorum_failure") OR scripted_quorum_risk IS NOT NULL OR evt_code IN ("1554","1571")
| stats latest(_time) as last_evt,
       values(evt_code) as codes,
       values(witness_evt) as witness_paths,
       values(quorum_resource_type) as quorum_modes,
       max(case(scripted_quorum_risk IN ("validation_bad","scripted_unreachable","metric_threshold"),1,0)) as scripted_alert,
       dc(host) as reporting_nodes
    by cluster_name
| eval posture=case(
    scripted_alert=1 OR mvfind(witness_paths,"quorum_failure")>0, "immediate_review",
    mvfind(witness_paths,"witness_transport")>0 OR mvfind(witness_paths,"witness_state")>0, "witness_flap",
    1=1, "informational")
| eval quorum_modes=mvdedup(quorum_modes)
| sort -last_evt
| table cluster_name, posture, quorum_modes, witness_paths, codes, scripted_alert, reporting_nodes, last_evt
```

**Field rationale:** evt_code aligns FailoverClustering/Operational IDs documented by Microsoft—avoid regex-only Message filtering across locales.

**Macro suggestion:** promote scripted JSON predicates (`match` clauses on validation_overall and witness_https_ok) into macros/azurestackhci_witness_risk so tuning rolls fleet-wide.

**Evidence pairing:** Correlate **Splunk posture tiles** with **Failover Cluster Manager** quorum screenshots archived inside change tickets.

**Heartbeat cadence reminder:** NetFT defaults (~five-second heartbeat cadence with ~ten-second tolerances on healthy fabrics) justify brief gaps during NIC resets—track rolling averages separately from quorum-loss paging.

Step 3 — **Validate against consoles**
(1) Failover Cluster Manager (`cluadmin.msc`) → Networks tab → verify Metrics ordering aligns with Test-Cluster suites covering heartbeat subnets.

(2) **Windows Admin Center** (**2410+**) quorum wizard outputs versus Splunk quorum_modes strings.

(3) Test-ClusterValidationLog review after automation—compare Splunk validation_bad timestamps with Learn guidance HTML severity tables ([Validate guidance](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure-stack/hci/manage/validate)).

(4) **Stretch deployments:** Get-SRPartnership synchronous thresholds (**≤5 ms** guidance) differentiate replication backlog chatter from quorum votes.

Step 4 — **Operationalise alerting**
(1) **Saved alert hci_quorum_hot** where posture equals immediate_review—throttle fifteen minutes per cluster_name unless evt_code **1554** repeats three times inside five minutes.

(2) Mirror **Azure Monitor** workbook tiles only when Splunk stays authoritative—avoid divergent paging paths.

(3) **Cause** narratives cite Set-ClusterQuorum transitions (Disk Witness → Cloud Witness) straight from Microsoft quorum docs ([manage quorum](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/failover-clustering/manage-cluster-quorum)).

Step 5 — **Troubleshooting patterns**

• **Silent pipe** — splunkd.log lacks Tailreader ACK for FailoverClustering/Operational → inspect inputs.conf checkpointInterval, wevtutil channel retention, MinimumRetention hygiene.

• **Unknown evt_code** — TA field collisions → fieldsummary WinEventLog subset; extend coalesce list after validating raw XML alongside rendered Message text.

• **EventCode 1566 without outage** — stale DNS conditional forwarders toward blob endpoints → Resolve-DnsName versus Test-NetConnection from observer VMs outside the cluster worker processes.

• **Duplicate scripted rows** — dedup macro keyed on cluster_name plus minute-bucketed _time prevents inflated reporting_nodes counts.

• **Test-Cluster XML advisory noise** — **Splunk intentionally ignores** benign Validation warnings—open ClusterValidationLog HTML attachments referenced via scripted report_path metadata.

• **CAU overlap validation_bad** — correlate Microsoft-Windows-ClusterAwareUpdating/Admin timelines using Get-CauRun identifiers exported offline when UI-only proof is mandatory.

• **Storage Replica backlog versus quorum** — SR saturation seldom mirrors quorum breach—ticket Cause routing distinguishes SR backlog follow-up from witness paging.

• **Disk-only quorum remnants** — legacy No Majority (Disk Only) layouts cannot infer vote survivability purely from Event **1564**—escalate when Disk Witness GUID disappears from inventory snapshots.

• **IPv6 preference jitter** — Get-ClusterNetworkRole mismatches versus NetFT adapter ordering foster phantom quorum instability until Set-ClusterNetwork priorities align ([stretch clustering guidance](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure-stack/hci/manage/stretch-clusters)).

• **TLS scanners** — enterprise scanners injecting RST toward blob endpoints yield witness_https_ok=false absent WinEvent correlates—expand scripted probes beyond **Splunk correlation windows**.

• **CSV latency masking NetFT** — pair **Splunk posture alerts** with Performance charts for clustered CSV Physical Disk counters when Hyper-V Live Migration saturates cluster networks.

• **HCI Insights divergence** — **portal greens while Splunk reds** often trace ComputerName mismatches tied to Failover virtual NETBIOS aliases—confirm `_raw` host fields against CMDB.
grandmaExplanation: We watch whether voting members stay healthy and whether the tie-break share stays reachable so the cluster picks who stays online—because broken vote math freezes apps before guests show symptoms.
hardware: Dell PowerEdge R760 / Lenovo ThinkAgile MX1021 / HPE ProLiant DL380 Gen11 HCI-ready builds / Cisco UCS C240 / Fujitsu PRIMERGY RX4770 M7 / DataON HCI-324
